nameMeanings.name: Adda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Alejandro, Alma Rosa, Carlos, Herlinda, Jorge, Luis, Miguel, Rodolfo, Romulo.
Basque: habitational name from a town called Anda in Araba/Álava province, Basque Country (Spain), named with Basque an ‘hill’. Compare De Anda .
Norwegian: habitational name from a southwestern farm name, possibly derived from and ‘duck’.

variants.name: De Anda
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Raul, Juan, Jesus, Carlos, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Eduardo, Lupe, Mario, Rodolfo.
Spanish: habitational name, formed with the preposition de ‘from’, for someone from a town called Anda (see Anda ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

nameMeanings.name: Goch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Pawel, Zuzanna, Zygmunt.
North German: habitational name from any of various minor places which get their names from an ancient Germanic element goch, gog ‘marsh, bog, fen’.
Polish: from a pet form of various personal names beginning with Go-, such as Gościsław.

variants.name: Gotch
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’; see Gooch 3.

nameMeanings.name: Angharad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old Celtic intensive prefix an- + cār ‘love’ + the noun suffix -ad. This was the name of the mother of the 12th-century chronicler Giraldus Cambrensis (‘Gerald the Welshman’). In the Mabinogi, Angharad Golden Hand at first rejects Peredur's suit, but later falls in love with him when he comes back as the unknown Mute Knight. The name has been strongly revived in Wales since the 1940s, borne, for example, by the actress Angharad Rees ( 1949-2012 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

en.lifeSummary: When Angharad Ferch Adda Goch was born about 1320, in Trevor, Llangollen, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Adda Goch ap Ieuaf of Trevor, was -4 and her mother, Angharad ferch Daffydd, was 1. She had at least 4 sons and 4 daughters with Iorwerth Ddu ap Ednyfed Gam. She died in Wales, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Angharad Ferch Adda Gochが約1320年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアランゴレントレボーで生まれた時，父Adda Goch ap Ieuaf of Trevorは-4歳，母Angharad ferch Daffyddは1歳であった。彼女はIorwerth Ddu ap Ednyfed Gamとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女はイギリスウェールズで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Angharad Ferch Adda Goch(이)가 대략 1320년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 랭렌 트레보르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adda Goch ap Ieuaf of Trevor(은)는 -4세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Angharad ferch Daffydd(은)는 1세였다. 그녀는 Iorwerth Ddu ap Ednyfed Gam(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 웨일스에서 사망했다.
